issues.title: Breadcrumbs missing ARIA nav landmark and breadcrumb list semantics
issues.description: The Breadcrumbs component renders plain divs and buttons without a nav landmark, aria-label, or ol/li breadcrumb list structure. Screen readers cannot identify this as navigation. Page acceptance criteria require axe-core zero critical violations and proper keyboard navigation.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component: BEFORE: The component renders a container div with button children and ChevronRight separators but uses no semantic nav element, no aria-label, no ordered list, and no aria-current on the last breadcrumb item. AFTER: Wrap the entire breadcrumb bar in a <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb"> element. Inside, use an <ol> with role="list" and style listStyle:'none', display:'flex', alignItems:'center', margin:0, padding:0. Each breadcrumb segment (including the root) should be an <li>. The last li should have aria-current="page" on its inner span/button. Separators (ChevronRight icons) should have aria-hidden="true". Apply the same pattern to the route-based Breadcrumbs export if it exists in the same file: wrap in nav with aria-label, use ol/li structure, and set aria-current="page" on the final crumb. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Toast error dismissal relies on 60s countdown without keyboard Escape support
issues.description: Error toasts auto-dismiss after 60 seconds with a countdown timer, but there is no keyboard shortcut (Escape) to dismiss them and no focus management. A user encountering an error toast must wait or find the tiny close button. Acceptance criteria require keyboard navigable UI where Escape closes modals and overlays.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component: BEFORE: Error toasts have a 60-second fallback auto-dismiss and a close button, but there is no keydown listener for Escape to dismiss toasts, and the toast container does not trap or manage focus. The close button lacks an aria-label. AFTER: 1) Add an aria-label="Dismiss notification" to the close/dismiss button inside ToastEntry. 2) Add a useEffect inside ToastEntry that listens for the Escape keydown event on document. When Escape is pressed and a toast is visible, call handleClose(toast.id) for the topmost toast. Use a ref to track if this toast is the most recent one (check if toast index is last). 3) On the toast container div, add role="alert" for error/warning severity and role="status" for success/info severity. 4) Add aria-live="assertive" for error toasts and aria-live="polite" for info/success toasts on the outer container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Breadcrumbs isMobile state uses window.innerWidth without resize listener cleanup or SSR guard
issues.description: The FolderBreadcrumbs component initializes isMobile with useState(() => window.innerWidth < 768) but the provided code snippet shows the start of a resize listener setup without verifying it has a cleanup return or handles SSR where window is undefined. Touch targets on breadcrumb buttons need minimum 44x44px per Apple HIG (page acceptance criteria).
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component: BEFORE: The isMobile state is initialized with window.innerWidth directly in useState initializer, and the crumbButtonStyle has minHeight:'44px' and minWidth:'44px' but there is no guarantee the resize listener has proper cleanup. The component may crash in SSR contexts. AFTER: 1) Change the useState initializer to use a safe check: useState(() => typeof window !== 'undefined' ? window.innerWidth < 768 : false). 2) Add a useEffect with a resize handler that calls setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 768), debounced with a 150ms timeout. Return a cleanup function that removes the event listener and clears the timeout. 3) On mobile (isMobile === true), truncate the breadcrumb trail to show only the parent and current item with an ellipsis button in between. When the ellipsis is tapped, expand to show the full trail. 4) Ensure all clickable breadcrumb segments have minHeight: '44px' and minWidth: '44px' with display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center' for proper touch targets. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Global skeleton loader component missing from Primitives for consistent loading states
issues.description: Page acceptance criteria mandate that every page shows skeleton loaders matching final layout geometry within 100ms of navigation, with pulse animation. The codebase lacks a reusable SkeletonLoader primitive in Primitives.tsx. Each page implements ad-hoc loading states or shows nothing, violating the global standard.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Primitives.tsx, add the following new exported components at the end of the file: BEFORE: The file exports Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag, and other primitives but has no SkeletonLoader, SkeletonText, SkeletonCircle, or SkeletonTable components. AFTER: Add four new exported components: 1) SkeletonLoader: accepts width (string, default '100%'), height (string, default '20px'), borderRadius (string, default '8px'), style (React.CSSProperties). Renders a div with background: 'linear-gradient(90deg, #E5E7EB 25%, #F3F4F6 50%, #E5E7EB 75%)', backgroundSize: '200% 100%', animation: 'skeleton-pulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite', the passed width/height/borderRadius, and a keyframe injected via a useEffect that adds a style tag with id 'skeleton-keyframes' to document.head if not already present. The keyframe: @keyframes skeleton-pulse { 0% { background-position: 200% 0 } 100% { background-position: -200% 0 } }. Add aria-hidden="true" and role="presentation". 2) SkeletonText: accepts lines (number, default 3) and gap (string, default '12px'). Renders lines number of SkeletonLoader divs with varying widths (100%, 100%, 75% for last line). 3) SkeletonCircle: accepts size (string, default '40px'). Renders SkeletonLoader with borderRadius: '50%' and width/height set to size. 4) SkeletonTable: accepts rows (number, default 5) and columns (number, default 4). Renders a header row of SkeletonLoader elements (height 16px, width varying per column) and then rows number of data rows (height 48px per row, gap 8px). All components typed with React.FC and proper CSSProperties. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No global command palette (Cmd+K) for keyboard power users
issues.description: Design standards specify Cmd+K for command palette, G+D for dashboard, G+R for RFIs, N for new item. No CommandPalette component exists. Enterprise users and PMs expect keyboard-driven navigation. This is a key differentiator vs Procore which has no command palette.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/CommandPalette.tsx, create a new file with the following: BEFORE: This file does not exist. There is no command palette or keyboard shortcut system in the app. AFTER: Create a CommandPalette component that: 1) Renders a modal overlay (position: fixed, inset: 0, zIndex: 9999, background: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)') with a centered dialog (max-width: 560px, width: '90%', background: colors.surface from theme, borderRadius: '12px', boxShadow: shadows.xl from theme, overflow: 'hidden'). 2) At the top, an input field (height: 48px, fontSize: 16px, border: none, padding: '0 16px', width: '100%', outline: 'none') with placeholder 'Search pages, actions, and commands...' and a Search icon (from lucide-react) on the left. 3) Below the input, a scrollable results list (max-height: 360px, overflow-y: auto). Each result item is a button with padding 12px 16px, display flex, gap 12px, align-items center, full width, hover background colors.backgroundSecondary. 4) Results are grouped by category: 'Pages' (Dashboard, RFIs, Submittals, Budget, Schedule, Daily Log, Punch List, etc. with their lucide icons), 'Actions' (New RFI, New Task, New Daily Log, etc.). Filter results by input text matching label. 5) Keyboard: ArrowUp/ArrowDown to navigate results, Enter to select, Escape to close. Track activeIndex state. Scroll active item into view. 6) The component accepts isOpen and onClose props. 7) Export a useCommandPalette hook that adds a global keydown listener for Meta+k (Mac) and Ctrl+k (Windows) that toggles isOpen state. Also handle G then D (dashboard), G then R (rfis), G then B (budget) with a two-key combo tracker using a ref for the first key and a 500ms timeout. 8) Use react-router-dom useNavigate for page navigation on result selection. 9) Import colors, spacing, typography, shadows, borderRadius, transitions from '../styles/theme'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Global Command Palette with Keyboard Shortcuts
invented_features.description: A Cmd+K powered command palette that allows PMs to search pages, create items, and navigate anywhere in under 2 seconds. Includes two-key combos (G+D for dashboard, G+R for RFIs) matching Linear and Figma patterns. No construction PM software has this. It makes SiteSync feel like developer-grade tooling applied to construction.
invented_features.implementation: New file src/components/CommandPalette.tsx with fuzzy search, keyboard navigation, and route integration. Wire into App.tsx with the useCommandPalette hook.

competitive_intel: Procore has no command palette, no keyboard shortcuts, and basic skeleton loaders. Their toast system is functional but not keyboard dismissible. Fieldwire has good mobile breadcrumbs but no desktop keyboard navigation. SiteSync can leapfrog both by shipping a Linear-quality command palette and WCAG AA compliant notification system. The skeleton loader system alone elevates perceived performance beyond anything in construction SaaS.
summary: The UI Design System module at 45/100 has a solid foundation with themed primitives (Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag) and a toast notification system, but lacks critical infrastructure that enterprise users expect. The five highest-impact issues are: (1) Breadcrumbs missing ARIA navigation semantics, violating accessibility acceptance criteria; (2) Toast notifications lacking keyboard dismiss and proper ARIA roles, making error recovery inaccessible; (3) Mobile breadcrumb truncation and resize handling incomplete, failing field-first requirements; (4) No reusable SkeletonLoader primitive, causing every page to implement inconsistent loading states; (5) No command palette for keyboard power users, missing a key differentiator from Procore. Fixing these five issues would push the module toward 60+ by establishing the infrastructure every other page depends on for loading states, accessibility, keyboard navigation, and mobile responsiveness.
